name: Rotation x Asymmetry Coupling
carving_goal: Determine if rotation and asymmetry are independent or coupled.
status: COMPLETE — 9/9 runs. First 7 in main session, last 2 backfilled via detached on-device script.

categorization.evidence: rot=60 x asym=+0.5 gives 3/5 HIGH (E=78,92,79,91,92), while rot=0 x asym=+0.5 gives 0/5 (all E≈79) and rot=120 x asym=+0.5 gives 1/5 (E=77,78,92,79,78). Rotation=60 is the most effective at pushing episodes across the HIGH boundary.
categorization.rot120_vs_rot60_at_boundary: rot=120 gave 1/5 HIGH vs rot=60 gave 3/5 HIGH at asym=+0.5. The C3 generator is NOT special — rot=60° is more effective. This further weakens Session 3's C3-specialness claim.
categorization.away_from_boundary: At asym=-0.5 and asym=0.0, all three rotations give similar HIGH rates (1-2/5). No coupling visible away from the boundary.

pre_registration_evaluation.independent_null: KILLED — rot=60 x asym=+0.5 differs from rot=0 x asym=+0.5
pre_registration_evaluation.asymmetry_dominates: PARTIAL — asymmetry is primary, rotation modulates at boundary
